verses.fhb_text: For behold, the day is coming, burning like a furnace, when all the arrogant and all evildoers will be stubble. The day that is coming shall set them ablaze, says our Father, the LORD of all heaven's armies, so that it will leave them neither root nor branch.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Day of judgment opens the closing oracle; YHWH-Tzva'ot resolves to the canonical Father apposition.

Renderings:
• "says our Father, the LORD of all heaven's armies" — YHWH-Tzva'ot compound; narrator-voice apposition per FHB-1.2-9 and FHB-1.2-27 Malachi convention
• "neither root nor branch" — preserve literal Hebrew metaphor

verses.fhb_text: But for you who fear my name, the sun of righteousness shall rise with healing in its wings. You shall go out leaping like calves released from the stall.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Father speaks in first person to those who honor his name; sun-of-righteousness Messianic image preserved.

Renderings:
• "fear my name" — Father self-references in first person per FHB-1.2-1 v4.3 grammatical agreement
• "sun of righteousness... with healing in its wings" — preserve Hebrew feminine pronoun matching shemesh; do not impose Christological gendering in body

verses.fhb_text: And you shall tread down the wicked, for they will be ashes under the soles of your feet, on the day when I act, says our Father, the LORD of all heaven's armies.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Father acts on his day; YHWH-Tzva'ot apposition repeats.

Renderings:
• "the day when I act" — Father self-references in first person
• "says our Father, the LORD of all heaven's armies" — apposition pattern continues from v.1

verses.fhb_text: Remember the law of Moses my servant, the statutes and rules that I commanded him at Horeb for all Israel.
verses.decision_notes: Headline: Father speaks; Moses named as servant. Routine prophetic injunction tying the closing oracle back to Sinai.

verses.fhb_text: Behold, I will send you Elijah the prophet before the great and awesome day of YHWH comes.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Elijah prophecy; day-of-YHWH eschatological compound preserved.

Renderings:
• "I will send you Elijah the prophet" — Father first-person; NT (Matt 11:14, 17:10-13; Luke 1:17) applies Elijah-figure to John the Baptist per FHB-1.2-2 — footnote at v.6 carries the bridge
• "the great and awesome day of YHWH" — fixed prophetic compound preserved per FHB-1.2-31 #1 explicit name-claim and #4 quoted prophetic formula; Father refers to his own day using his name

verses.fhb_text: And he will turn the hearts of fathers to their children and the hearts of children to their fathers, lest I come and strike the land with utter destruction.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Old Testament closes on our Father's heart for family restoration through the Elijah-figure who prepares the way for Jesus.

Renderings:
• "turn the hearts of fathers to their children" — preserve as written; explicit filial-restoration language
• "lest I come and strike the land" — Father first-person
• "with utter destruction" — herem rendered with the severity the Hebrew carries without imposing technical-ban vocabulary
verses.footnote: The Old Testament closes with our Father's heart for family restoration. Matthew 11:14 and 17:10-13 identify John the Baptist as the Elijah-figure preparing the way for Jesus.

chapter_notes: Malachi 4 is the closing chapter of the Old Testament (Hebrew 3:19-24). The Father speaks throughout in first person — promising judgment on the arrogant, healing for those who honor his name, the Elijah-figure who will come, and the restoration of family hearts. YHWH-Tzva'ot apposition (vv.1, 3) follows the established Malachi convention from FHB-1.2-27. The Elijah prophecy (v.5) and family-restoration closing (v.6) are anchored to the New Testament's application to John the Baptist preparing the way for Jesus — one footnote at v.6 carries the bridge for both verses, introducing a new canon-wide category (ot_closes_family_restoration).
